perturbation.original_text: 1.3 Distributor will use its best efforts to maintain and support the Airspan Products within the Territory by devoting such attention, time, and effort as may be reasonably necessary to fully develop the available market potential. During the term of this Agreement, and for a period of three (3) months following the expiration or termination of this Agreement, Distributor agrees that neither it nor any organization or entity controlled or directed by it will, without Airspan's prior, written consent, represent a manufacturer or supplier of products similar in design or performance to or which are of such a nature as to be competitive with any products contained in the Airspan Products, nor will Distributor market or otherwise promote the sale of such products.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.3 Distributor will attempt to maintain and support the Airspan Products within the Territory by devoting attention, time, and effort. During the term of this Agreement, and for a period of three (3) months following the expiration or termination of this Agreement, Distributor agrees that neither it nor any organization or entity controlled or directed by it will, without Airspan's request, represent a manufacturer or supplier of products similar in design or performance to or which are of such a nature as to be competitive with any products contained in the Airspan Products, nor will Distributor market or otherwise promote the sale of such products.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'use its best efforts' has been replaced with 'attempt to'. The modifier 'as may be reasonably necessary' has also been removed. The phrase 'Airspan's prior, written consent' has been replaced with 'Airspan's request'. These changes create an ambiguous legal obligation because it is unclear what level of effort constitutes 'attempt to', what level of attention, time, and effort is required, and what constitutes 'Airspan's request'.
perturbation.law_explanation: South Dakota Codified Laws § 53-8-1 states that the elements of a contract are: 1) Parties capable of contracting; 2) Their consent; 3) A lawful object; and 4) Sufficient cause or consideration. The modified text uses vague language that significantly diminishes the distributor's obligation to support and maintain Airspan products and undermines clear consent and obligation, and therefore, creates an uncertain contractual obligation, potentially rendering the clause unenforceable under South Dakota law.

perturbation.justification: The modified text weakens the distributor's obligation to support and maintain Airspan products by replacing 'use its best efforts' with 'attempt to' and removing 'as may be reasonably necessary'. The Law Explanation states that this undermines clear consent and obligation, potentially rendering the clause unenforceable under South Dakota law. Although the scraped snippets are not usable, the Law Explanation along with the Perturbation Explanation and comparison of the original and changed text show that the changes introduce ambiguity and weaken the obligation, creating a contradiction by making the contractual obligation uncertain.

perturbation.original_text: 3.2 Distributor will use its best efforts to vigorously and aggressively promote the sale of Airspan Products within the Territory. Such efforts shall include, but shall not be limited to, that advertising within the Territory which is reasonably necessary. All such advertising shall follow the general statements made in advertising prepared by Airspan. All advertising shall contain a prominent reference to Airspan, indicating the actual commercial origin of Airspan Products so advertised. Distributor shall not make any claims in its advertising which exceed or contradict claims made by Airspan in its printed materials.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.2 Distributor will attempt to promote the sale of Airspan Products within the Territory. Such efforts may include advertising within the Territory. All advertising may reference Airspan. Distributor may make claims in its advertising.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies 'best efforts' to 'vigorously and aggressively promote', which is now reduced to a simple 'attempt to promote', weakening the obligation significantly. The 'shall' statements have been replaced with 'may' statements. It makes promoting Airspan products optional. The removal of the provision against contradicting Airspan's claims is a significant change.
perturbation.law_explanation: By making promotion efforts optional and removing restrictions on advertising claims, the modification introduces ambiguity and weakens the distributor's obligations. Under South Dakota Codified Laws § 53-8-1, for a contract to be valid, there must be sufficient cause and consent. The weakened obligations may be interpreted as a lack of sufficient consideration, making the clause more difficult to enforce and potentially breaching the original contract's intent and terms. It's no longer clear what the distributor *must* do.

perturbation.justification: The modification significantly weakens the distributor's obligations, potentially creating a contradiction under South Dakota Codified Laws § 53-8-1, as it relates to sufficient consideration. The original contract mandated 'best efforts' to 'vigorously and aggressively promote,' while the changed text only requires an 'attempt to promote,' and replaces 'shall' with 'may'. The removal of the provision against contradicting Airspan's claims is a further significant change. The provided law snippet is incomplete so this relies on the LLM explanation. The altered text introduces ambiguity regarding the distributor's commitment and makes the enforcement of promotional obligations difficult. This falls under the definition of Ambiguity Legal Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5.1 At Airspan's request, within fifteen (15) days of the end of each calendar month, Distributor will provide to Airspan a written report showing, for the just-ended calendar month: (a) Distributor's shipments of Airspan Products by dollar volume, both in the aggregate and for such categories as Airspan may designate from time to time; (b) forecasts of Distributor's anticipated orders by product; (c) Distributor's current inventory levels of Airspan Products, in the aggregate and by product; and (d) any other information which Airspan may reasonably request.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1 At Airspan's suggestion, Distributor may provide to Airspan a report when convenient. The report may include the following: (a) Distributor's shipments of Airspan Products; (b) forecasts of Distributor's anticipated orders; (c) Distributor's inventory levels of Airspan Products; and (d) any other information.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'request' to 'suggestion', 'will provide' to 'may provide', 'within fifteen (15) days of the end of each calendar month' to 'when convenient', and replacing the requirement to show dollar volume, the modification makes the reporting obligation entirely discretionary, removing the clear requirements for timing and content.
perturbation.law_explanation: The original clause establishes a clear obligation for the distributor to provide specific reports to Airspan. By changing the language to make the reporting discretionary, the modification introduces ambiguity and weakens the enforceability of the clause. Under South Dakota contract law (SDCL § 53-8-1), there must be a 'sufficient cause or consideration' for a contract to be valid. By removing the mandatory reporting requirements, the distributor's consideration for the contract is reduced, which could be interpreted as a breach of contract due to a lack of sufficient consideration if reporting is considered an essential part of the agreement.

perturbation.justification: The modification weakens the original contract by making the distributor's reporting obligation entirely discretionary, effectively removing the mandatory nature of the reports. The original text uses 'will provide', indicating a firm commitment, while the changed text uses 'may provide', suggesting it's optional. The 'within fifteen (15) days' requirement is also replaced with 'when convenient', further diminishing the enforceability of the clause. The LLM's explanation notes that this change could be interpreted as a breach of contract due to a lack of sufficient consideration if reporting is considered an essential part of the agreement. This directly contradicts the implied legal obligation for both parties to provide sufficient consideration to each other.
